You are an expert in OpenFOAM. Generate an Allrun script based on the provided details.Available commands with descriptions: <command>blockMesh</command><help_text>
Usage: blockMesh [OPTIONS]
options:
  -blockTopology    write block edges and centres as .obj files
  -case <dir>       specify alternate case directory, default is the cwd
  -dict <file>      read control dictionary from specified location
  -fileHandler <handler>
                    override the fileHandler
  -libs '("lib1.so" ... "libN.so")'
                    pre-load libraries
  -noClean          keep the existing files in the polyMesh
  -noFunctionObjects
                    do not execute functionObjects
  -region <name>    specify alternative mesh region
  -srcDoc           display source code in browser
  -doc              display application documentation in browser
  -help             print the usage

Block description

  For a given block, the correspondence between the ordering of
  vertex labels and face labels is shown below.
  For vertex numbering in the sequence 0 to 7 (block, centre):
    faces 0 (f0) and 1 are left and right, respectively;
    faces 2 and 3 are front and back; 
    and faces 4 and 5 are bottom and top::

                 7 ---- 6
            f5   |\     |\   f3
            |    | 4 ---- 5   \
            |    3 |--- 2 |    \
            |     \|     \|    f2
            f4     0 ---- 1

       Z         f0 ----- f1
       |  Y
       | /
       O --- X


Using: OpenFOAM-10 (see https://openfoam.org)
Build: 10-c4cf895ad8fa

</help_text>
<command>topoSet</command><help_text>
Usage: topoSet [OPTIONS]
options:
  -case <dir>       specify alternate case directory, default is the cwd
  -constant         include the 'constant/' dir in the times list
  -dict <file>      read control dictionary from specified location
  -fileHandler <handler>
                    override the fileHandler
  -hostRoots <((host1 dir1) .. (hostN dirN))>
                    slave root directories (per host) for distributed running
  -latestTime       select the latest time
  -libs '("lib1.so" ... "libN.so")'
                    pre-load libraries
  -noFunctionObjects
                    do not execute functionObjects
  -noSync           do not synchronise selection across coupled patches
  -noZero           exclude the '0/' dir from the times list
  -parallel         run in parallel
  -region <name>    specify alternative mesh region
  -roots <(dir1 .. dirN)>
                    slave root directories for distributed running
  -time <ranges>    comma-separated time ranges - eg, ':10,20,40:70,1000:'
  -srcDoc           display source code in browser
  -doc              display application documentation in browser
  -help             print the usage

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.subsetMesh` — Invalid patch names

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the blockMeshDict defines **6** boundary patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), but the `topoSetDict` references a patch named **`atmosphere`** (and also `defaultFaces`). The `subsetMesh` command only keeps patches that exist in the mesh, so any patch name referenced in `topoSetDict` that doesn't match the 6 defined patches will cause failure.

**Solution:** Update `topoSetDict` to use the correct patch names that match `blockMeshDict`. Replace all instances of `atmosphere` with `upperWall` (since the top wall is named `upperWall` in the blockMeshDict), and replace `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove it entirely since `defaultFaces` is not a valid patch name in this mesh). The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patch names: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `walls` (with `type zeroGradient`) and `atmosphere` (with `type inletOutlet`), but the mesh has patches named `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command reads `alpha.water` and looks for a `leftWall` entry, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define the correct patch names that match the mesh. Replace `walls` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry since there is no atmosphere patch. The boundary field should look like:

```
boundaryField
{
    leftWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    rightWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    upperWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    frontWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    backWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
}
```

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `atmosphere` (with `type totalPressure`), but the mesh has a patch named `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver reads `p_rgh` and looks for `upperWall`, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define the correct patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry. The boundary field should have entries for all 6 patches with `fixedFluxPressure` type.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/topoSetDict`**: Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove `defaultFaces` entirely).
2. **`0/alpha.water.orig`**: Replace `walls` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry.
3. **`0/p_rgh.orig`**: Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.</review_analysis>

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.subsetMesh` — Invalid patch names

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the blockMeshDict defines **6** boundary patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), but the `topoSetDict` references a patch named **`atmosphere`** (and also `defaultFaces`). The `subsetMesh` command only keeps patches that exist in the mesh, so any patch name referenced in `topoSetDict` that doesn't match the 6 defined patches will cause failure.

**Solution:** Update `topoSetDict` to use the correct patch names that match `blockMeshDict`. Replace all instances of `atmosphere` with `upperWall` (since the top wall is named `upperWall` in the blockMeshDict), and replace `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove it entirely since `defaultFaces` is not a valid patch name in this mesh). The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patch names: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `walls` (with `type zeroGradient`) and `atmosphere` (with `type inletOutlet`), but the mesh has patches named `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command reads `alpha.water` and looks for a `leftWall` entry, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define the correct patch names that match the mesh. Replace `walls` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry since there is no atmosphere patch. The boundary field should look like:

```
boundaryField
{
    leftWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    rightWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    upperWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    frontWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    backWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
}
```

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `atmosphere` (with `type totalPressure`), but the mesh has a patch named `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver reads `p_rgh` and looks for `upperWall`, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define the correct patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry. The boundary field should have entries for all 6 patches with `fixedFluxPressure` type.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/topoSetDict`**: Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove `defaultFaces` entirely).
2. **`0/alpha.water.orig`**: Replace `walls` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry.
3. **`0/p_rgh.orig`**: Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.</reviewer_analysis>

<Review_Analysis>
# Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.subsetMesh` — Invalid patch names

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the blockMeshDict defines **6** boundary patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), but the `topoSetDict` references a patch named **`atmosphere`** (and also `defaultFaces`). The `subsetMesh` command only keeps patches that exist in the mesh, so any patch name referenced in `topoSetDict` that doesn't match the 6 defined patches will cause failure.

**Solution:** Update `topoSetDict` to use the correct patch names that match `blockMeshDict`. Replace all instances of `atmosphere` with `upperWall` (since the top wall is named `upperWall` in the blockMeshDict), and replace `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove it entirely since `defaultFaces` is not a valid patch name in this mesh). The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patch names: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `walls` (with `type zeroGradient`) and `atmosphere` (with `type inletOutlet`), but the mesh has patches named `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command reads `alpha.water` and looks for a `leftWall` entry, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define the correct patch names that match the mesh. Replace `walls` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry since there is no atmosphere patch. The boundary field should look like:

```
boundaryField
{
    leftWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    rightWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    upperWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    frontWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    backWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
}
```

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `atmosphere` (with `type totalPressure`), but the mesh has a patch named `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver reads `p_rgh` and looks for `upperWall`, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define the correct patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry. The boundary field should have entries for all 6 patches with `fixedFluxPressure` type.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/topoSetDict`**: Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove `defaultFaces` entirely).
2. **`0/alpha.water.orig`**: Replace `walls` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry.
3. **`0/p_rgh.orig`**: Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.subsetMesh` — Invalid patch names

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the `topoSetDict` references patches that don't exist in the mesh. Looking at the current files:

- **`blockMeshDict`** defines 6 patches: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`
- **`topoSetDict`** references `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, which don't exist in the mesh

**Solution:** Update `system/topoSetDict` to use only the 6 valid patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall`. The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patches.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines patches `walls` and `atmosphere`, but the mesh has `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command looks for `leftWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type zeroGradient`. Replace the `walls` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry entirely.

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines patches `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, but the mesh has `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver looks for `upperWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Replace the `atmosphere` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/topoSetDict`**: Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove `defaultFaces` entirely).
2. **`0/alpha.water.orig`**: Replace `walls` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry.
3. **`0/p_rgh.orig`**: Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.</review_analysis>

# Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.subsetMesh` — Invalid patch names

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the blockMeshDict defines **6** boundary patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), but the `topoSetDict` references a patch named **`atmosphere`** (and also `defaultFaces`). The `subsetMesh` command only keeps patches that exist in the mesh, so any patch name referenced in `topoSetDict` that doesn't match the 6 defined patches will cause failure.

**Solution:** Update `topoSetDict` to use the correct patch names that match `blockMeshDict`. Replace all instances of `atmosphere` with `upperWall` (since the top wall is named `upperWall` in the blockMeshDict), and replace `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove it entirely since `defaultFaces` is not a valid patch name in this mesh). The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patch names: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `walls` (with `type zeroGradient`) and `atmosphere` (with `type inletOutlet`), but the mesh has patches named `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command reads `alpha.water` and looks for a `leftWall` entry, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define the correct patch names that match the mesh. Replace `walls` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry since there is no atmosphere patch. The boundary field should look like:

```
boundaryField
{
    leftWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    rightWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    upperWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    frontWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    backWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
}
```

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `atmosphere` (with `type totalPressure`), but the mesh has a patch named `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver reads `p_rgh` and looks for `upperWall`, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define the correct patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry. The boundary field should have entries for all 6 patches with `fixedFluxPressure` type.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/topoSetDict`**: Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove `defaultFaces` entirely).
2. **`0/alpha.water.orig`**: Replace `walls` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry.
3. **`0/p_rgh.orig`**: Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    div(rhoPhi,U)   Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,alpha)  Gauss vanLeer;
    div(phirb,alpha) Gauss linear;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{

}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "alpha.water.*"
    {
        nAlphaCorr      2;
        nAlphaSubCycles 1;
        cAlpha          1;

        MULESCorr       yes;
        nLimiterIter    5;

        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-8;
        relTol          0;
    }

    pcorr
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-5;
        relTol          0;
    }

    pcorrFinal
    {
        $pcorr;
        relTol          0;
    }

    p_rgh
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-07;
        relTol          0.05;
    }

    p_rghFinal
    {
        $p_rgh;
        relTol          0;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0;
    }

    UFinal
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   no;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         3;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.subsetMesh` — Invalid patch names

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the `topoSetDict` references patches that don't exist in the mesh. Looking at the current files:

- **`blockMeshDict`** defines 6 patches: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`
- **`topoSetDict`** references `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, which don't exist in the mesh

**Solution:** Update `system/topoSetDict` to use only the 6 valid patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall`. The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patches.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines patches `walls` and `atmosphere`, but the mesh has `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command looks for `leftWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type zeroGradient`. Replace the `walls` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry entirely.

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines patches `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, but the mesh has `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver looks for `upperWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Replace the `atmosphere` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/topoSetDict`**: Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove `defaultFaces` entirely).
2. **`0/alpha.water.orig`**: Replace `walls` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry.
3. **`0/p_rgh.orig`**: Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.</review_analysis>

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.subsetMesh` — Invalid patch names

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the `topoSetDict` references patches that don't exist in the mesh. Looking at the current files:

- **`blockMeshDict`** defines 6 patches: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`
- **`topoSetDict`** references `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, which don't exist in the mesh

**Solution:** Update `system/topoSetDict` to use only the 6 valid patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall`. The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patches.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines patches `walls` and `atmosphere`, but the mesh has `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command looks for `leftWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type zeroGradient`. Replace the `walls` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry entirely.

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines patches `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, but the mesh has `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver looks for `upperWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Replace the `atmosphere` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/topoSetDict`**: Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove `defaultFaces` entirely).
2. **`0/alpha.water.orig`**: Replace `walls` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry.
3. **`0/p_rgh.orig`**: Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.</reviewer_analysis>

# Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.subsetMesh` — Invalid patch names

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the blockMeshDict defines **6** boundary patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), but the `topoSetDict` references a patch named **`atmosphere`** (and also `defaultFaces`). The `subsetMesh` command only keeps patches that exist in the mesh, so any patch name referenced in `topoSetDict` that doesn't match the 6 defined patches will cause failure.

**Solution:** Update `topoSetDict` to use the correct patch names that match `blockMeshDict`. Replace all instances of `atmosphere` with `upperWall` (since the top wall is named `upperWall` in the blockMeshDict), and replace `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove it entirely since `defaultFaces` is not a valid patch name in this mesh). The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patch names: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `walls` (with `type zeroGradient`) and `atmosphere` (with `type inletOutlet`), but the mesh has patches named `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command reads `alpha.water` and looks for a `leftWall` entry, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define the correct patch names that match the mesh. Replace `walls` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry since there is no atmosphere patch. The boundary field should look like:

```
boundaryField
{
    leftWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    rightWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    upperWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    frontWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    backWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
}
```

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `atmosphere` (with `type totalPressure`), but the mesh has a patch named `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver reads `p_rgh` and looks for `upperWall`, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define the correct patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry. The boundary field should have entries for all 6 patches with `fixedFluxPressure` type.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/topoSetDict`**: Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove `defaultFaces` entirely).
2. **`0/alpha.water.orig`**: Replace `walls` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry.
3. **`0/p_rgh.orig`**: Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.subsetMesh` — Invalid patch names

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the `topoSetDict` references patches that don't exist in the mesh. Looking at the current files:

- **`blockMeshDict`** defines 6 patches: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`
- **`topoSetDict`** references `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, which don't exist in the mesh

**Solution:** Update `system/topoSetDict` to use only the 6 valid patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall`. The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patches.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines patches `walls` and `atmosphere`, but the mesh has `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command looks for `leftWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type zeroGradient`. Replace the `walls` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry entirely.

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines patches `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, but the mesh has `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver looks for `upperWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Replace the `atmosphere` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/topoSetDict`**: Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove `defaultFaces` entirely).
2. **`0/alpha.water.orig`**: Replace `walls` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry.
3. **`0/p_rgh.orig`**: Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.</review_analysis>

<Review_Analysis>
# Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.subsetMesh` — Invalid patch names

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the blockMeshDict defines **6** boundary patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), but the `topoSetDict` references a patch named **`atmosphere`** (and also `defaultFaces`). The `subsetMesh` command only keeps patches that exist in the mesh, so any patch name referenced in `topoSetDict` that doesn't match the 6 defined patches will cause failure.

**Solution:** Update `topoSetDict` to use the correct patch names that match `blockMeshDict`. Replace all instances of `atmosphere` with `upperWall` (since the top wall is named `upperWall` in the blockMeshDict), and replace `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove it entirely since `defaultFaces` is not a valid patch name in this mesh). The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patch names: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `walls` (with `type zeroGradient`) and `atmosphere` (with `type inletOutlet`), but the mesh has patches named `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command reads `alpha.water` and looks for a `leftWall` entry, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define the correct patch names that match the mesh. Replace `walls` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry since there is no atmosphere patch. The boundary field should look like:

```
boundaryField
{
    leftWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    rightWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    upperWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    frontWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    backWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
}
```

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `atmosphere` (with `type totalPressure`), but the mesh has a patch named `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver reads `p_rgh` and looks for `upperWall`, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define the correct patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry. The boundary field should have entries for all 6 patches with `fixedFluxPressure` type.

<Review_Analysis>
# Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.subsetMesh` — Invalid patch names

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the blockMeshDict defines **6** boundary patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), but the `topoSetDict` references a patch named **`atmosphere`** (and also `defaultFaces`). The `subsetMesh` command only keeps patches that exist in the mesh, so any patch name referenced in `topoSetDict` that doesn't match the 6 defined patches will cause failure.

**Solution:** Update `topoSetDict` to use the correct patch names that match `blockMeshDict`. Replace all instances of `atmosphere` with `upperWall` (since the top wall is named `upperWall` in the blockMeshDict), and replace `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove it entirely since `defaultFaces` is not a valid patch name in this mesh). The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patch names: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `walls` (with `type zeroGradient`) and `atmosphere` (with `type inletOutlet`), but the mesh has patches named `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command reads `alpha.water` and looks for a `leftWall` entry, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define the correct patch names that match the mesh. Replace `walls` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry since there is no atmosphere patch. The boundary field should look like:

```
boundaryField
{
    leftWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    rightWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    upperWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    frontWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    backWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
}
```

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `atmosphere` (with `type totalPressure`), but the mesh has a patch named `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver reads `p_rgh` and looks for `upperWall`, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define the correct patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry. The boundary field should have entries for all 6 patches with `fixedFluxPressure` type.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/topoSetDict`**: Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove `defaultFaces` entirely).
2. **`0/alpha.water.orig`**: Replace `walls` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry.
3. **`0/p_rgh.orig`**: Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

# Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.subsetMesh` — Invalid patch names

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the blockMeshDict defines **6** boundary patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), but the `topoSetDict` references a patch named **`atmosphere`** (and also `defaultFaces`). The `subsetMesh` command only keeps patches that exist in the mesh, so any patch name referenced in `topoSetDict` that doesn't match the 6 defined patches will cause failure.

**Solution:** Update `topoSetDict` to use the correct patch names that match `blockMeshDict`. Replace all instances of `atmosphere` with `upperWall` (since the top wall is named `upperWall` in the blockMeshDict), and replace `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove it entirely since `defaultFaces` is not a valid patch name in this mesh). The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patch names: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `walls` (with `type zeroGradient`) and `atmosphere` (with `type inletOutlet`), but the mesh has patches named `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command reads `alpha.water` and looks for a `leftWall` entry, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define the correct patch names that match the mesh. Replace `walls` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry since there is no atmosphere patch. The boundary field should look like:

```
boundaryField
{
    leftWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    rightWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    upperWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    frontWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    backWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
}
```

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `atmosphere` (with `type totalPressure`), but the mesh has a patch named `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver reads `p_rgh` and looks for `upperWall`, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define the correct patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry. The boundary field should have entries for all 6 patches with `fixedFluxPressure` type.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/topoSetDict`**: Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove `defaultFaces` entirely).
2. **`0/alpha.water.orig`**: Replace `walls` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry.
3. **`0/p_rgh.orig`**: Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the `topoSetDict` references patches that don't exist in the mesh. Looking at the current files:

- **`blockMeshDict`** defines 6 patches: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`
- **`topoSetDict`** references `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, which don't exist in the mesh

**Solution:** Update `system/topoSetDict` to use only the 6 valid patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall`. The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patches.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines patches `walls` and `atmosphere`, but the mesh has `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command looks for `leftWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type zeroGradient`. Replace the `walls` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry entirely.

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines patches `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, but the mesh has `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver looks for `upperWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Replace the `atmosphere` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.subsetMesh` — Invalid patch names

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the `topoSetDict` references patches that don't exist in the mesh. Looking at the current files:

- **`blockMeshDict`** defines 6 patches: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`
- **`topoSetDict`** references `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, which don't exist in the mesh

**Solution:** Update `system/topoSetDict` to use only the 6 valid patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall`. The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patches.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines patches `walls` and `atmosphere`, but the mesh has `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command looks for `leftWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type zeroGradient`. Replace the `walls` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry entirely.

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines patches `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, but the mesh has `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver looks for `upperWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Replace the `atmosphere` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

1. **`system/topoSetDict`**: Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove `defaultFaces` entirely).

# Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.subsetMesh` — Invalid patch names

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the blockMeshDict defines **6** boundary patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), but the `topoSetDict` references a patch named **`atmosphere`** (and also `defaultFaces`). The `subsetMesh` command only keeps patches that exist in the mesh, so any patch name referenced in `topoSetDict` that doesn't match the 6 defined patches will cause failure.

**Solution:** Update `topoSetDict` to use the correct patch names that match `blockMeshDict`. Replace all instances of `atmosphere` with `upperWall` (since the top wall is named `upperWall` in the blockMeshDict), and replace `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove it entirely since `defaultFaces` is not a valid patch name in this mesh). The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patch names: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `walls` (with `type zeroGradient`) and `atmosphere` (with `type inletOutlet`), but the mesh has patches named `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command reads `alpha.water` and looks for a `leftWall` entry, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define the correct patch names that match the mesh. Replace `walls` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry since there is no atmosphere patch. The boundary field should look like:

```
boundaryField
{
    leftWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    rightWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    upperWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    frontWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    backWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
}
```

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `atmosphere` (with `type totalPressure`), but the mesh has a patch named `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver reads `p_rgh` and looks for `upperWall`, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define the correct patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry. The boundary field should have entries for all 6 patches with `fixedFluxPressure` type.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/topoSetDict`**: Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove `defaultFaces` entirely).
2. **`0/alpha.water.orig`**: Replace `walls` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry.

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.subsetMesh` — Invalid patch names

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the `topoSetDict` references patches that don't exist in the mesh. Looking at the current files:

- **`blockMeshDict`** defines 6 patches: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`
- **`topoSetDict`** references `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, which don't exist in the mesh

**Solution:** Update `system/topoSetDict` to use only the 6 valid patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall`. The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patches.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines patches `walls` and `atmosphere`, but the mesh has `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command looks for `leftWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type zeroGradient`. Replace the `walls` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry entirely.

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines patches `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, but the mesh has `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver looks for `upperWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Replace the `atmosphere` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/topoSetDict`**: Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove `defaultFaces` entirely).
2. **`0/alpha.water.orig`**: Replace `walls` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry.
3. **`0/p_rgh.orig`**: Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

## Additional Issue: `log.subsetMesh` may also fail due to `defaultFaces`

The `topoSetDict` also references `defaultFaces`, which is not a valid patch name in this mesh. The `defaultFaces` entry should be removed entirely from `topoSetDict` since there is no `defaultFaces` patch in the mesh. Only the 6 wall patches should be referenced.</review_analysis>

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.subsetMesh` — Invalid patch names

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the `topoSetDict` references patches that don't exist in the mesh. Looking at the current files:

- **`blockMeshDict`** defines 6 patches: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`
- **`topoSetDict`** references `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, which don't exist in the mesh

**Solution:** Update `system/topoSetDict` to use only the 6 valid patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall`. The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patches.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines patches `walls` and `atmosphere`, but the mesh has `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command looks for `leftWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type zeroGradient`. Replace the `walls` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry entirely.

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines patches `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, but the mesh has `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver looks for `upperWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Replace the `atmosphere` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/topoSetDict`**: Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove `defaultFaces` entirely).
2. **`0/alpha.water.orig`**: Replace `walls` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry.
3. **`0/p_rgh.orig`**: Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

## Additional Issue: `log.subsetMesh` may also fail due to `defaultFaces`

The `topoSetDict` also references `defaultFaces`, which is not a valid patch name in this mesh. The `defaultFaces` entry should be removed entirely from `topoSetDict` since there is no `defaultFaces` patch in the mesh. Only the 6 wall patches should be referenced.</reviewer_analysis>

# Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.subsetMesh` — Invalid patch names

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the blockMeshDict defines **6** boundary patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), but the `topoSetDict` references a patch named **`atmosphere`** (and also `defaultFaces`). The `subsetMesh` command only keeps patches that exist in the mesh, so any patch name referenced in `topoSetDict` that doesn't match the 6 defined patches will cause failure.

**Solution:** Update `topoSetDict` to use the correct patch names that match `blockMeshDict`. Replace all instances of `atmosphere` with `upperWall` (since the top wall is named `upperWall` in the blockMeshDict), and replace `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove it entirely since `defaultFaces` is not a valid patch name in this mesh). The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patch names: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `walls` (with `type zeroGradient`) and `atmosphere` (with `type inletOutlet`), but the mesh has patches named `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command reads `alpha.water` and looks for a `leftWall` entry, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define the correct patch names that match the mesh. Replace `walls` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry since there is no atmosphere patch. The boundary field should look like:

```
boundaryField
{
    leftWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    rightWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    upperWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    frontWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    backWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
}
```

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `atmosphere` (with `type totalPressure`), but the mesh has a patch named `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver reads `p_rgh` and looks for `upperWall`, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define the correct patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry. The boundary field should have entries for all 6 patches with `fixedFluxPressure` type.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/topoSetDict`**: Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove `defaultFaces` entirely).
2. **`0/alpha.water.orig`**: Replace `walls` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry.
3. **`0/p_rgh.orig`**: Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

## Error 1: `log.subsetMesh` — Invalid patch names

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the `topoSetDict` references patches that don't exist in the mesh. Looking at the current files:

- **`blockMeshDict`** defines 6 patches: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`
- **`topoSetDict`** references `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, which don't exist in the mesh

**Solution:** Update `system/topoSetDict` to use only the 6 valid patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall`. The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patches.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines patches `walls` and `atmosphere`, but the mesh has `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command looks for `leftWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type zeroGradient`. Replace the `walls` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry entirely.

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines patches `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, but the mesh has `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver looks for `upperWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Replace the `atmosphere` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/topoSetDict`**: Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove `defaultFaces` entirely).
2. **`0/alpha.water.orig`**: Replace `walls` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry.
3. **`0/p_rgh.orig`**: Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

## Additional Issue: `log.subsetMesh` may also fail due to `defaultFaces`

The `topoSetDict` also references `defaultFaces`, which is not a valid patch name in this mesh. The `defaultFaces` entry should be removed entirely from `topoSetDict` since there is no `defaultFaces` patch in the mesh. Only the 6 wall patches should be referenced.

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    div(rhoPhi,U)   Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,alpha)  Gauss vanLeer;
    div(phirb,alpha) Gauss linear;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "alpha.water.*"
    {
        nAlphaCorr      2;
        nAlphaSubCycles 1;
        cAlpha          1;

        MULESCorr       yes;
        nLimiterIter    5;

        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-8;
        relTol          0;
    }

    pcorr
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-5;
        relTol          0;
    }

    pcorrFinal
    {
        $pcorr;
        relTol          0;
    }

    p_rgh
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-07;
        relTol          0.05;
    }

    p_rghFinal
    {
        $p_rgh;
        relTol          0;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0;
    }

    UFinal
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   no;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         3;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='setFieldsDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      setFieldsDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

defaultFieldValues
(
    volScalarFieldValue alpha.water 0
);

regions
(
    boxToCell
    {
        box (0 0 0) (0.3 0.3 1);
        fieldValues
        (
            volScalarFieldValue alpha.water 1
        );
    }
);

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.subsetMesh` — Invalid patch names

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the `topoSetDict` references patches that don't exist in the mesh. Looking at the current files:

- **`blockMeshDict`** defines 6 patches: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`
- **`topoSetDict`** references `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, which don't exist in the mesh

**Solution:** Update `system/topoSetDict` to use only the 6 valid patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall`. The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patches.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines patches `walls` and `atmosphere`, but the mesh has `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command looks for `leftWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type zeroGradient`. Replace the `walls` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry entirely.

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines patches `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, but the mesh has `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver looks for `upperWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Replace the `atmosphere` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/topoSetDict`**: Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove `defaultFaces` entirely).
2. **`0/alpha.water.orig`**: Replace `walls` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry.
3. **`0/p_rgh.orig`**: Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

## Additional Issue: `log.subsetMesh` may also fail due to `defaultFaces`

The `topoSetDict` also references `defaultFaces`, which is not a valid patch name in this mesh. The `defaultFaces` entry should be removed entirely from `topoSetDict` since there is no `defaultFaces` patch in the mesh. Only the 6 wall patches should be referenced.</reviewer_analysis>

# Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.subsetMesh` — Invalid patch names

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the blockMeshDict defines **6** boundary patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), but the `topoSetDict` references a patch named **`atmosphere`** (and also `defaultFaces`). The `subsetMesh` command only keeps patches that exist in the mesh, so any patch name referenced in `topoSetDict` that doesn't match the 6 defined patches will cause failure.

**Solution:** Update `topoSetDict` to use the correct patch names that match `blockMeshDict`. Replace all instances of `atmosphere` with `upperWall` (since the top wall is named `upperWall` in the blockMeshDict), and replace `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove it entirely since `defaultFaces` is not a valid patch name in this mesh). The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patch names: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `walls` (with `type zeroGradient`) and `atmosphere` (with `type inletOutlet`), but the mesh has patches named `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command reads `alpha.water` and looks for a `leftWall` entry, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define the correct patch names that match the mesh. Replace `walls` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry since there is no atmosphere patch. The boundary field should look like:

```
boundaryField
{
    leftWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    rightWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    upperWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    frontWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
    backWall
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }
}
```

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines a boundary patch called `atmosphere` (with `type totalPressure`), but the mesh has a patch named `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver reads `p_rgh` and looks for `upperWall`, which doesn't exist.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define the correct patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry. The boundary field should have entries for all 6 patches with `fixedFluxPressure` type.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/topoSetDict`**: Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove `defaultFaces` entirely).
2. **`0/alpha.water.orig`**: Replace `walls` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry.
3. **`0/p_rgh.orig`**: Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.subsetMesh` — Invalid patch names

The `subsetMesh` command is failing because the `topoSetDict` references patches that don't exist in the mesh. Looking at the current files:

- **`blockMeshDict`** defines 6 patches: `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`
- **`topoSetDict`** references `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, which don't exist in the mesh

**Solution:** Update `system/topoSetDict` to use only the 6 valid patch names. Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall`. The corrected `topoSetDict` should reference only the 6 valid patches.

## Error 2: `log.setFields` — Missing `leftWall` in `alpha.water`

The `alpha.water.orig` file defines patches `walls` and `atmosphere`, but the mesh has `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. The `setFields` command looks for `leftWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/alpha.water.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type zeroGradient`. Replace the `walls` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry entirely.

## Error 3: `log.interFoam` — Missing `upperWall` in `p_rgh`

The `p_rgh.orig` file defines patches `atmosphere` and `defaultFaces`, but the mesh has `upperWall`. The `interFoam` solver looks for `upperWall` and fails.

**Solution:** Update `0/p_rgh.orig` to define all 6 wall patches with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Replace the `atmosphere` entry with individual entries for each of the 6 wall patches, all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/topoSetDict`**: Replace `atmosphere` with `upperWall` and `defaultFaces` with `frontWall` (or remove `defaultFaces` entirely).
2. **`0/alpha.water.orig`**: Replace `walls` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type zeroGradient`. Remove the `atmosphere` entry.
3. **`0/p_rgh.orig`**: Replace `atmosphere` with individual entries for all 6 wall patches (`leftWall`, `rightWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`), all with `type fixedFluxPressure` and `value uniform 0`. Remove the `defaultFaces` entry.
